title: Bioavailability Potentiation
description: Piperine alkaloid in Black Pepper actively inhibits intestinal and hepatic glucuronidation (via UDP-glucuronosyltransferase inhibition) that otherwise rapidly degrades Curcumin. Combining them increases Curcumin's systemic bioavailability by up to 2,000%, achieving therapeutic tissue concentrations.
clinicalAdvice: Highly recommended clinical synergy. Always combine Turmeric preparations with a pinch of freshly ground Black Pepper at the bedside to ensure therapeutic anti-inflammatory efficacy.
